title: The 5-Step Pipeline: Discover, Enrich, Score, Personalize, Send

questions.question: Why is the order of pipeline stages critical, as illustrated by Alex's $40K mistake?

options.text: The tools only work in a specific order
Enriching without discovery criteria wastes money on bad-fit prospects — the pipeline must filter before enriching
Sending must always come last for compliance reasons
Personalization only works on scored leads

questions.explanation: Alex enriched every prospect without a proper discovery stage, wasting $4,000 on enrichment for unqualified leads. The lesson teaches that pipeline architecture isn't about having all five stages — it's about having them in the right order so each stage only processes data worth processing.

questions.question: What is the 'Pipeline Multiplier Effect'?

options.text: Each tool in the pipeline doubles your output
A 30% improvement at each of the 5 stages compounds to 3.7x total improvement
The pipeline gets faster each time you run it

questions.explanation: The Pipeline Multiplier Effect means improvements compound across stages. A 30% improvement at each of the 5 stages (Discover, Enrich, Score, Personalize, Send) doesn't add up to 150% — it multiplies to approximately 3.7x total improvement by stage 5.

questions.question: At the Score stage, what happens to prospects scoring below 5?

options.text: They receive the same outreach as higher-scored prospects
They are moved to nurture or disqualified — they don't proceed to Personalize
They are automatically deleted from the CRM
They get extra enrichment to improve their score

questions.explanation: The quality gate at the Score stage is: Score 5 or above? If no, move to nurture or disqualify. Only Tier A and B leads (score 5+) proceed to the Personalize stage. This prevents wasting personalization effort and sending capacity on low-probability prospects.

questions.question: You should enrich every prospect you discover, regardless of whether they match your ICP, because more data is always better.
questions.explanation: False. Enriching 10,000 bad-fit prospects costs the same as enriching 500 perfect-fit ones, but only the second group converts. The Discovery stage must filter with ICP criteria before passing prospects to the Enrich stage. Each stage has a quality gate to prevent wasted resources downstream.

questions.question: Map your current outreach process to the 5-step pipeline (Discover, Enrich, Score, Personalize, Send). Which stages are you doing well? Which are missing or out of order? What is the first change you would make?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the response maps their actual process to all 5 stages, honestly identifies gaps or ordering problems, and proposes a specific first improvement. Look for understanding of quality gates between stages and the importance of filtering before enriching.
